other_observers: No one whose name I know; There have been many other observers since the bird was first discovered, but I do not know the names of the original observers.
description: I observed a medium-sized, chunky, heron-type bird with a short neck; gray body, black face with a distinctive white patch on its cheek and a yellow stripe along its crown; a few white nuptial feathers extended back from its crown; thick dagger-type black bill.Behaviors: The night-heron was perched on a dead branch extending over the water on the west side of an island hosting a mixed heron breeding colony. In my observation it did not move much.Call: none heardPlumage: adult breeding
